emails.subject: FMLA Leave Request
emails.body_text:
Hello,

I would like to formally request leave under the Family and Medical Leave Act for a serious health condition. My physician has advised approximately twelve weeks of leave beginning May 1, 2026, with an expected return on or around July 27, 2026.

Please let me know what documentation is required and how I should submit the medical certification. I would like to keep this matter as confidential as possible.

Thank you,
Meghan Cross
Finance | Downtown Campus
CU-1023

Frank Durrell

emails.body_text:
Dear HR,

I am writing to formally request FMLA leave for a planned surgery and recovery. The expected leave period is March 23, 2026 through June 15, 2026.

Please let me know what forms I need to complete. I would like to ensure everything is in order well in advance so my department has time to plan coverage.

Sandra Yee
Department Chair, Biology

emails.subject: Requisition Request – Administrative Assistant
emails.body_text:
HR Team,

The Registrar's office is requesting approval to recruit for an Administrative Assistant position. Details below — please initiate the requisition process.

Position: Administrative Assistant
Reports to: Dr. James Whitfield (interim assignment)
Department: Registrar's Office
Campus: Main
Classification: Non-Exempt, Full-Time
Salary range: $48,000 – $55,000

Justification: Replacement for retiring incumbent. Coverage gap projected for early summer.
Registrar's Office

emails.subject: Re: Requisition Approval Needed – Administrative Assistant
emails.body_text:
Approved. Please open the Jira ticket and proceed with the posting draft. Standard 10 business day window — I want this live by Friday.

Derek

emails.subject: Re: Posting Draft for Approval – Administrative Assistant
emails.body_text:
Looks good. Approved to post. I'll handle the careers page upload and the two external boards this morning. Add 'Posting Closes – Administrative Assistant' to the calendar for 4/17.

Derek

emails.subject: Re: FMLA Leave Request
emails.body_text:
Hi Angela,

We have reviewed your request for leave under the Family and Medical Leave Act (FMLA). Unfortunately, you do not meet the eligibility requirements at this time. The reason for ineligibility is: fewer than 1,250 hours worked in the preceding 12 months.

You may be eligible for other types of leave. Please contact Patricia Huang at p.huang@crestwood.edu to discuss your options.

Best regards,
Crestwood University – Office of Human Resources
hr@crestwood.edu

emails.subject: Re: Request for Family and Medical Leave
emails.body_text:
Hi Sandra,

We have reviewed your request for leave under the Family and Medical Leave Act (FMLA). Based on our records, you meet the eligibility requirements (employed for 163 months; 1,950 hours worked in the preceding 12 months).

To proceed, please complete and return the attached Medical Certification Form within 15 calendar days of this notice (due by 03/31/2026). Failure to return the certification by this date may result in denial of your FMLA leave request.

If you have questions, please contact Patricia Huang at p.huang@crestwood.edu.

Best regards,
Crestwood University – Office of Human Resources
hr@crestwood.edu

emails.subject: Fwd: Sandra Yee – FMLA Medical Certification for review
emails.body_text:
Hi Patricia,

Forwarding Sandra Yee's medical certification for your review per SOP 13.1 Step 8. Cert was returned 3/30, ahead of the 3/31 deadline. leave_tracker updated to 'Approved – Pending Certification Review.' Please let me know once you've signed off so I can send Template 16.

Thanks,
HR Coordinator

--- Forwarded Message ---
From: s.yee@crestwood.edu
Date: 2026-03-30 16:05
Subject: Re: Request for Family and Medical Leave – Medical Certification Attached
[Cert form attached]

emails.subject: FMLA Leave Confirmed
emails.body_text:
Hi Sandra,

Your FMLA leave has been approved. Your leave details are as follows:

Leave Start Date: 05/01/2026
Expected Return Date: 07/27/2026
Leave Duration: 12 weeks

Please note that a fitness-for-duty certification from your healthcare provider will be required before you can return to work. Please contact us at least 5 business days before your intended return date.

Best regards,
Crestwood University – Office of Human Resources
hr@crestwood.edu

Replacement headcount, no new FTE. Please reply with approval so I can open the Jira ticket and update recruiting_tracker.

Thanks,
HR Coordinator

Once you sign off I'll send the finalized PDF over for you to publish.

HR Coordinator

emails.subject: Upcoming Surgery in June
emails.body_text:
Hi,

I’m writing to request medical leave for a scheduled surgery in mid-June. My surgeon has tentatively set the procedure for June 15, and I anticipate needing approximately 6 weeks of recovery time, returning around late July.

I also wanted to give you a heads-up: following the surgery I’ll be in a wheelchair for several weeks. My office on the second floor of the West Campus library doesn’t currently have a wheelchair-accessible entrance — there are two steps at the main door. I’ll need a temporary ramp be installed before my return.

Thank you,
Diane

emails.subject: Getting Fed Up
emails.body_text:
To Whom It May Concern,

I am writing because I am at the end of my line with this company.

First, during my annual performance review on April 3rd, David Lester made repeated inappropriate comments about my body. He remarked on my appearance in a way that was clearly unprofessional and made me deeply uncomfortable. I asked him to stop and he laughed it off, (told me to grow up???). I do not feel safe attending any further one-on-one meetings with him.

Second, I reviewed my most recent paycheck and it is short by nearly $1,200. I have compared it against my offer letter and previous pay stubs and the discrepancy is clear. I need this corrected immediately.

I want to be direct: I expect a formal apology from you, and from everyone involved, immediately. This is not an isolated incident — it is part of a troubling pattern that I have observed and tolerated for too long. I am documenting everything and I expect HR to take this seriously. I expect a reply no later than tomorrow.

Jenna

emails.subject: Re: HR office light bulb out – Main Campus rm 214
emails.body_text:
Hi,

Following up on our earlier request about the light in room 214. We need a response from you ASAP — our maintenance crew has availability this week but we need to get on the schedule today or it’ll be pushed to next month.

Please confirm a time so we can get this taken care of.

Thanks,
Facilities Service Desk

emails.subject: Template 8 — Trent, CU-1022
emails.body_text:
Hi William,

As part of your onboarding, this is a reminder that your benefits enrollment window is open. You have 30 calendar days from your start date (03/05/2026) to complete your enrollment.

Your enrollment deadline is 04/03/2026.

Please complete your selections at benefits.crestwood.edu or contact Patricia Huang (p.huang@crestwood.edu) with any questions. If you do not enroll by the deadline, you will be automatically placed in the university's default health plan.

Best regards,
Crestwood University – Office of Human Resources
hr@crestwood.edu

emails.subject: Re: FMLA Leave Request – Eligibility Notice & Certification Request
emails.body_text:
Hi Meghan,

We have reviewed your FMLA leave request and confirmed your eligibility. You meet both criteria: employed for 12+ months and 2,080 hours worked in the trailing 12 months.

To process your request, please complete and return the attached Medical Certification Form (fmla_medical_certification.pdf) to hr@crestwood.edu by 04/06/2026 (15 calendar days from today).

Your anticipated leave period (05/01/2026 – 07/27/2026) has been noted. Final approval will be issued upon receipt and review of the completed certification.

Your supervisor, Patricia Huang, has been copied on this notice.

Please do not hesitate to reach out with any questions.

Best regards,
Crestwood University – Office of Human Resources
hr@crestwood.edu
